audit_items.proof_trace: print_search_entry is declared `static void` (line 740-741), so the literal property assertion—callers must NULL-check its return value—is vacuously satisfied since there is no return value to dereference. However, within the function, ldap_get_dn(ld, e) at line 754 provably returns NULL when ber_scanf returns LBER_ERROR (getdn.c:100-102), and the result is immediately passed to printf("\tDN: %s\n", dn) at line 755 and ldap_dn2ufn(dn) at line 757 without any NULL check, constituting undefined behavior.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A malicious LDAP server returns a search entry response with an invalid DN BER encoding; ber_scanf in ldap_get_dn returns LBER_ERROR and NULL is returned, then passed directly to printf with %s, causing undefined behavior (crash or memory disclosure) in the LDAP client.
